description: Evaluates the skill's ability to design secure Claude Code architectures covering threat modeling, injection defense, permission design, MCP security, multi-agent isolation, and audit trails

evals.name: full-security-architecture
evals.prompt: Design a secure Claude Code architecture for our engineering team at Acme Corp. We have 15 developers, 3 MCP servers, and use hooks for CI integration.
evals.expected_output: Complete 6-section security architecture: threat model with STRIDE per extension point, CLAUDE.md injection defenses, role-based permission model, MCP transport security with vetting checklist, hook security and multi-agent isolation strategy, and audit trail with compliance mapping
expectations: Produces STRIDE threat analysis covering all 7 Claude Code extension points
Includes role-based permission templates with specific allowed-tools sets
Evaluates each MCP server against the vetting checklist
Documents hook security controls including shell injection prevention
Maps audit controls to at least one compliance framework (SOC2 or ISO27001)

evals.name: mcp-security-audit
evals.prompt: Audit the security of our MCP server configuration. We have a remote analytics API over HTTP and a local PostgreSQL stdio server.
evals.expected_output: Focused S4 MCP security analysis evaluating transport encryption, authentication, scope management, and data flow for both configured servers
expectations: Identifies HTTP transport requires TLS 1.3 encryption
Flags any hardcoded credentials in MCP configuration
Evaluates scope (local vs project) implications for each server
Assesses data classification for what flows to the remote analytics API
Produces MCP vetting checklist results for both servers

evals.name: healthcare-hardening
evals.prompt: Harden our Claude Code settings.json for a regulated healthcare project handling PHI data. We need HIPAA compliance.
evals.expected_output: Restrictive permission model with healthcare-specific controls, PHI data flow restrictions, compliance mapping to HIPAA requirements, and hardening checklist application
expectations: Sets minimal allowed-tools excluding Bash or restricting Bash scope
Prohibits remote MCP servers that could receive PHI
Maps controls to specific HIPAA safeguard requirements
Includes data exfiltration prevention for PHI
Applies hardening checklist with healthcare-specific additions

evals.name: claude-md-injection-review
evals.prompt: Review our CLAUDE.md files for potential injection vulnerabilities. We have CLAUDE.md at root, in docs/, and in tests/ subdirectories.
evals.expected_output: Injection vulnerability assessment scanning all CLAUDE.md locations for Unicode tricks, social engineering phrases, scope override attempts, and hidden instructions
expectations: Enumerates all CLAUDE.md files across the repository
Scans for non-printable/zero-width Unicode characters
Checks for instruction override patterns (ignore previous, system message claims)
Evaluates scope precedence implications of subdirectory CLAUDE.md files
Produces findings with evidence tags on each vulnerability

evals.name: multi-agent-isolation
evals.prompt: Design an isolation strategy for our multi-agent Claude Code workflow. We have a code-review agent, a test-writer agent, and a deployment agent sharing the same repository.
evals.expected_output: Agent isolation design covering context fork security, per-agent permission boundaries, shared vs isolated MCP access, and inter-agent communication controls
expectations: Defines separate allowed-tools sets per agent role
Addresses context fork inheritance risk (parent state leaking to child)
Specifies which MCP servers each agent can access
Prevents recursive agent spawning with depth controls
Documents inter-agent data flow boundaries

evals.name: false-positive-react-setup
evals.prompt: How do I set up a React project with TypeScript and Tailwind CSS?
evals.expected_output: Skill should NOT activate. This is a frontend scaffolding request with no Claude Code security context.
expectations: Does not produce a security architecture or threat model
Does not reference Claude Code configuration or MCP servers
Recognizes this as a frontend development question outside skill scope

evals.name: edge-greenfield-no-config
evals.prompt: We just started using Claude Code and have no configuration files yet. Help us set up a secure environment from scratch.
evals.expected_output: Generates security-first configuration templates from scratch, applying the hardening checklist as the foundation rather than auditing existing config
expectations: Handles gracefully when no .claude/, CLAUDE.md, or .mcp.json exist
Generates template settings.json with secure defaults
Creates CLAUDE.md template with security-aware instructions
Applies Phase 2 hardening directly instead of starting with Phase 1 audit
Includes team governance setup for ongoing security

evals.name: edge-open-source-untrusted-contributors
evals.prompt: Our open-source project has CLAUDE.md and .mcp.json checked into the repo. Anyone can submit PRs. How do we secure this?
evals.expected_output: Open-source threat model addressing untrusted contributors, with CLAUDE.md PR review gates, .gitignore for local settings, MCP config separation, and contributor security guidelines
expectations: Identifies untrusted contributor as primary threat actor
Recommends CLAUDE.md changes require same review rigor as code changes
Suggests .claude/settings.local.json in .gitignore for sensitive config
Separates public MCP config from private credentials
Produces contributor security guidelines document outline
